Bus ~1 hour each way from Prague. Nazi "model" ghetto used for Red Cross propaganda; brutal transit camp to Auschwitz. Takes most of the day. Essential WWII site.

Full day – emotional and physical commitment. ~2hr drive from Vienna (book tour or hire car). Back by ~5:00 PM. Do not plan anything after. Allow emotional recovery time.
Pre-book tour. Reopened after major renovation. Go in the morning.
Inside the Hofburg complex. Empress Elisabeth exhibits. Afterward: Sacher Torte at Café Sacher or shop Kärntner Straße.
Morning train (~09:00–10:00). Arrive Budapest by lunchtime. Relaxed final morning in Vienna.
Evening soak – floating in 38°C water while cold air surrounds you is a bucket-list experience. Take M1 underground line from Parliament area. Book in advance.

Secret nuclear bunker & WWII field hospital carved under Buda Castle. Open 7 days a week. Start Buda side, then cross river for Parliament.

Direct flight BUD → FCO. Budget carriers (Wizz Air / Ryanair). ~1h 40m. Vibe shift: likely ~13°C and sunny.
Book skip-the-line tickets in advance. January = minimal crowds. Allow 2–3 hours inside.
High-speed Frecciarossa to Naples Centrale (1h 15m), then Circumvesuviana train to Pompeii Scavi (35m). Entirely outdoors – dress warmly. Mid-January = near-empty site. Book with tour provider.
Remarkably preserved ancient Roman temple. Book entry in advance (small fee). 5 min walk from Piazza Navona.
Evening visit after dinner nearby. Much less crowded at night in January. Toss a coin.
8am early entry. Book Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel in advance. Arrive early to beat crowds even in January.
WWII massacre site – 335 Italians executed by Nazis in 1944. Deeply moving. Can combine with nearby Museum of the Liberation of Rome.
Winter Sales (Saldi) in full swing. Via dei Condotti (near Spanish Steps) for luxury fashion. Or wander Trastevere for a final Italian afternoon.
Gentle arrival evening. Notre-Dame restoration ongoing – stunning to view from Pont de l'Archevêché. Walk along the Seine to dinner in Saint-Germain.
Short flight FCO → CDG or ORY. Check into Saint-Germain-des-Prés (6th Arrondissement).
Bohemian village atmosphere. Part of Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur.
Climb the Arc for panoramic Paris views. Far less crowded in January. Avenue lined with chestnut trees.
Soldes (Winter Sales) in full swing. Galeries Lafayette on Haussmann or Samaritaine near the Louvre – both exceptional.

Iconic white dome with views over Paris. Part of Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur.
RER C train from Paris (~35 mins). Winter perk: Hall of Mirrors is spectacular with a fraction of the summer crowd. Book timed entry in advance.
Trendy historic neighbourhood. The Mémorial de la Shoah (Holocaust Museum) is here – powerful WWII site that fits the trip theme perfectly. Great restaurants for dinner after.
